title: The Red Queen Gödel Machine: Co-Evaluating Agents and Their Evaluators
summary: The Red Queen Gödel Machine introduces a co-evolutionary framework where AI agents and their evaluation mechanisms continuously improve through mutual optimization. Rather than treating evaluation as a static benchmark, the architecture enables evaluators to evolve alongside agents, creating an adversarial yet cooperative feedback ecosystem that drives sustained capability growth. This co-evaluation paradigm addresses evaluator overfitting, reward hacking, and stagnation while supporting open-ended self-improvement. The framework advances research in autonomous AI evolution, self-improving systems, and recursive agent optimization, providing a foundation for more robust and adaptive intelligent systems.
category: Self-Improving AI + Agent Evaluation + Recursive Optimization + Open-Ended Intelligence

title: Autodata: An Agentic Data Scientist for High-Quality Synthetic Data Generation
summary: AutoData introduces an autonomous agentic framework that automates the end-to-end process of synthetic data creation through iterative planning, generation, evaluation, and refinement. Rather than relying on manually engineered datasets, AutoData employs AI agents to understand task requirements, curate data distributions, generate synthetic examples, and continuously assess quality through feedback loops. This agentic approach significantly improves data diversity, realism, and task alignment while reducing human intervention. The framework provides a scalable foundation for generating high-quality synthetic datasets for training, benchmarking, and evaluating next-generation AI systems across multiple domains.
category: Synthetic Data + Agentic AI + Data Generation + AI Evaluation

title: JetSpec: Breaking the Scaling Ceiling of Speculative Decoding with Parallel Tree Drafting
summary: JetSpec introduces a novel speculative decoding architecture that overcomes traditional scaling limitations by leveraging parallel tree-based drafting strategies. Instead of generating speculative tokens sequentially, JetSpec constructs multiple candidate decoding paths simultaneously, enabling efficient exploration of diverse token trajectories and maximizing hardware utilization. This parallel tree drafting framework significantly improves inference throughput while maintaining generation quality, offering a scalable approach for accelerating large language model inference. JetSpec advances the state of efficient AI serving infrastructure and provides a practical pathway toward production-scale deployment of frontier language models.
category: LLM Inference + Speculative Decoding + Systems Optimization + AI Infrastructure

title: Reasoning Quality Emerges Early: Data Curation for Reasoning Models
summary: This work demonstrates that high-quality reasoning capabilities emerge primarily from careful data curation rather than solely from model scale or extensive post-training optimization. The research introduces systematic methodologies for constructing, filtering, and optimizing reasoning datasets, showing that strategically curated data substantially improves reasoning performance during early training phases. By emphasizing data quality, diversity, and cognitive complexity, the framework offers new insights into developing more efficient and capable reasoning models while reducing computational requirements. The findings provide important implications for the design of next-generation reasoning-centric foundation models.
category: Reasoning Models + Data Curation + Training Data + AI Research

title: AtomMem: Building Simple and Effective Memory System for LLM Agents via Atomic Facts
summary: AtomMem introduces a memory architecture that transforms unstructured interactions into atomic facts, enabling LLM agents to store, retrieve, update, and reason over long-term knowledge with greater precision and efficiency. Instead of maintaining memory as large conversation histories or embeddings alone, AtomMem decomposes information into structured factual units that can be incrementally managed and queried. This atomic-fact approach improves memory consistency, retrieval accuracy, scalability, and long-horizon agent performance, providing a practical foundation for persistent memory systems in next-generation autonomous AI agents.
category: Agent Memory + Long-Term Memory + Knowledge Management + Memory Architecture

title: Compositional Skill Routing for LLM Agents: Decompose, Retrieve, and Compose
summary: Compositional Skill Routing introduces a framework that enables LLM agents to dynamically solve complex tasks by decomposing objectives into reusable sub-skills, retrieving the most relevant capabilities from a skill repository, and composing them into executable workflows. Instead of relying on monolithic prompts or fixed tool chains, the architecture promotes modular reasoning, skill reuse, and adaptive task execution. By enabling agents to assemble task-specific solutions from a growing library of learned competencies, the framework improves scalability, generalization, and efficiency while advancing the development of more capable and flexible agent ecosystems.
category: Agent Architecture + Skill Composition + Agent Orchestration + Modular Reasoning

title: Loopcraft: The Art of Loop Engineering
summary: Loopcraft introduces a new architectural paradigm where AI systems are built through the deliberate composition of iterative execution loops rather than isolated prompt-response interactions. The framework positions planning, reasoning, execution, reflection, evaluation, memory, and coordination loops as the foundational building blocks of agentic systems. By stacking and orchestrating these loops across different timescales, developers can create adaptive AI systems that continuously learn, self-correct, recover from failures, and pursue long-running objectives. The approach represents a shift from workflow-centric design to loop-centric system engineering, enabling more resilient, autonomous, and production-grade AI agents capable of sustained reasoning and continuous improvement.
category: Loop Engineering + Agent Architecture + Autonomous Systems + AI Systems Design + Continuous Improvement Systems

title: Omnigent: The Control Plane for Enterprise Agent Ecosystems
summary: Omnigent is an open-source meta-harness that sits above existing agent frameworks and coding agents, enabling organizations to combine, control, and share heterogeneous agents through a unified abstraction layer. It introduces policy-driven governance, budget controls, permission management, sandboxed execution, multi-agent composition, and real-time collaborative workflows. By decoupling orchestration and control from individual agent harnesses, Omnigent enables interoperable, enterprise-scale agent ecosystems while reducing framework lock-in and operational complexity.
category: Agent Infrastructure + Agent Orchestration + Multi-Agent Systems + AI Governance

title: Continual Learning Bench (CL-Bench): Evaluating Frontier AI Systems in Stateful Environments
summary: CL-Bench introduces a comprehensive benchmark for measuring whether frontier AI systems can learn from experience, accumulate knowledge, transfer insights across tasks, and continuously improve within persistent stateful environments. The benchmark exposes a critical gap between model capability and adaptive intelligence, revealing that even advanced AI systems struggle with long-horizon learning, knowledge consolidation, and sustained performance improvement over time.
category: Continual Learning + Adaptive AI + Agent Evaluation
